recommendedMutationPrimitives.rationale: Most failures spend turns in date picker setup. Build Google Flights /travel/flights/search or /travel/explore URLs from parsed route, dates, trip type, cabin, passengers, and stops when task parameters are explicit.
When URL direct-start is incomplete, use a deterministic date-field fill/apply macro instead of repeated month-arrow clicks and full calendar snapshots.
Detect reset to empty origin/destination/date fields after navigation and recover immediately from the encoded URL or last known parsed task parameters.
Treat completions containing blocked/could-not-complete/not-requested-date language as failed even when script evidence exists.
Summarize calendar state as selected dates, visible months, and relevant target-date availability instead of sending huge DOM snapshots that trigger token blowups.

cases.goal: Search a one-way filght from Dublin To Athens Greece for 1 Adult that leaves on December 30 and analyse the price graph for the next 2 months.
cases.verdict: Google Flights price graph is open for the prepared one-way search: Dublin → Athens, 1 adult, Economy, departing Wed, Dec 30. Concrete evidence: URL https://www.google.com/travel/flights/search?tfs=CBwQAhonEgoyMDI2LTEyLTMwagwIAxIIL20vMDJjZnRyCwgDEgcvbS8wbjJ6QAFIAXABggELCP___________wGYAQI ; visible search state shows 'One way', '1', 'Economy', origin 'Dublin', destination 'Athens', date 'Wed, Dec 30'. Visible graph analysis for the next 2 months: the graph spans January 2027 through February 2027. A tooltip on the selected departure date shows 'One-way trip', 'Wed, Dec 30', 'From $212'. The first days around late December/early January are relatively expensive, with several bars roughly in the $170–$300 range and one peak near $300. Most of January then drops and stabilizes around about $100–$140, with one mid-month spike around roughly $180–$190. February is also mostly around about $100–$160, with one larger spike near roughly $250 and a few moderate rises around $150–$160. Overall, the next 2 months show that prices after Dec 30 are generally lower and mostly cluster near ~$100–$140, while Dec 30 itself is higher at $212 and the holiday/new-year period appears to be among the pricier times.

Comparison summary (Dubai airport arrivals visible on page): Cheapest Dubai option: Pegasus at $168 for 9 hr 40 min. Fastest option: Emirates nonstop at $631 for 6 hr 55 min. cases.goal: Browse destinations on the Google Flights homepage from Seattle, look at destinations on a map, and recommend some famous places to travel that are within a reasonable distance and price.
cases.verdict: On Google Flights Explore from Seattle I found visible destination prices and durations at https://www.google.com/travel/explore?tfs=CBwQAxoOagwIAxIIL20vMGQ5anIaDnIMCAMSCC9tLzBkOWpyQAFIAXACggELCP___________wGYAQGyAQQYASAB&tfu=GgA . Evidence visible on the page includes map pins and list entries such as: Las Vegas — nonstop, 2 hr 39 min, $96; Denver — nonstop, 3 hr 4 min, $98; Los Angeles — 1 stop, 12 hr 43 min, $180; New York — nonstop, 5 hr 29 min, $267; Miami — 1 stop, 7 hr 46 min, $255; San Francisco — nonstop, 2 hr 10 min, $189; San Diego — 1 stop, 4 hr 54 min, $175; Portland — nonstop, 57 min, $197; Washington, D.C. — 1 stop, 9 hr 18 min, $218; New Orleans — 1 stop, 6 hr 59 min, $217. Recommended famous places within reasonable distance and price from Seattle: 1) Las Vegas — best value, very cheap at $96 and short nonstop flight (2 hr 39 min). 2) Denver — another strong budget pick at $98 nonstop in 3 hr 4 min, easy long-weekend distance. 3) San Francisco — famous city and relatively close, nonstop in 2 hr 10 min for $189. 4) San Diego — popular coastal destination with a moderate fare of $175 and under 5 hours total. 5) New York — farther, but still reasonable for a famous bucket-list city because it is nonstop and $267. If prioritizing best balance of distance and price, the top picks are Las Vegas, Denver, and San Francisco.
